A DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF MORELAND IN SOUTH CENTRAL COWETA COUNTY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ALEX STEPHENS AND GORDON ROADS. THE 200 YARD WIDE TORNADO THEN STRENGTHENED QUICKLY TO AN EF1 IN A RESIDENTIAL AREA WHERE 10 HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND DOZENS OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED AFTER A MILE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GORDON ROAD AND MARTIN MILL ROAD.

DAMAGE TO 8-10 RESIDENTIAL PROPERTIES ALONG THE 700-1000 BLOCK OF GORDON ROAD OFF HWY 16. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE AT INTERSECTION WITH MARTIN MILL ROAD. MINOR DAMAGE...IF ANY...TO THE HOMES...WITH NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.

DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG GA HIGHWAY 128 JUST NORTH OF OGLETHORPE...THEN STRENGTHED TO AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR FOUR POINTS WHERE A HOUSE WAS DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ESE CAUSING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ALONG MENNONITE SCHOOL ROAD JUST NORTH OF GA HIGHWAY 26...WHERE TWO LARGE CHICKEN HOUSES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND A THIRD SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. THE TORNADO LIFTED AS AN EF0 NEAR FIVE POINTS.
911 REPORT FROM VOLUNTEER FIRE FIGHTER...POSSIBLE TORNADO...TREES DOWNED. NO FURTHER DETAILS.
NUMEROUS TREES AND WIRES DOWN...GENERALLY IN THE UNADILLA AREA. NO ADDITIONAL DETAILS HAVE BEEN PROVIDED AT THIS TIME.

DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN DOOLY COUNTY JUST NORTHWEST OF PINEHURST AND FOLLOWED A SPORADIC 5.5-MILE LONG ESE PATH. APPROXIMATELY 15 LARGE TREES WERE DOWN OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO...AND ONE POWER POLE. NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED.
1 TREE DOWN OFF HWY 87 S IN EASTMAN.

THE WATER LEVEL AT A USGS STREAM GAGE EXCEEDED FLOOD STAGE OF 12 FEET ON THE LOOKOUT CREEK AT CREEK ROAD. MINOR FLOODING ALSO OCCURRED ALONG THE CREEK AT SELLS LANE IN TRENTON AND MASON ROAD NEAR RISING FAWN.

THE WATER LEVEL AT A USGS STREAM GAGE EXCEEDED FLOOD STAGE OF 12 FEET ON THE LOOKOUT CREEK AT CREEK ROAD. MINOR FLOODING IS ALSO OCCURRING ALONG THE CREEK AT SELLS LANE IN TRENTON AND MASON ROAD NEAR RISING FAWN.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN FLOYD COUNTY AROUND 1012 PM TIME ESTIMATED JUST WEST OF THE MAPLEWOOD SUBDIVISION IN ROME. THE TORNADO TRAVELED 3.25 MILES...WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS AND A MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 95 MPH. THE GREATEST DAMAGE WAS ALONG KINGSTON HIGHWAY WHERE THE ENTIRE ROOF WAS RIPPED OFF A MANUFACTURED HOME. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. IN ADDITION...A FEW POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED.
FLOYD COUNTY 911 RELAYED REPORTS OF SEVERAL DOWNED TREES AND POWERLINES RESULTING IN POWER OUTAGES TO THE KINGSTON HIGHWAY AND FREEMAN FERRY ROAD AREA.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF-3 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN HARALSON COUNTY AT APPROXIMATELY 809 PM NEAR CROSS ROADS CHURCH ROAD AND TALLAPOOSA HWY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ACROSS HARALSON COUNTY AND INTO PAULDING COUNTY WHERE IT LIFTED NEAR MCCULRE TRL AND MCCLURE DR AROUND 850 PM. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 29 MILES AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. SOME OF THE WORST DAMAGE IN HARALSON COUNTY WAS ALONG BETHLEHEM CHURCH ROAD WHERE A HOUSE COLLAPSED AND A REPAIR SHOP WAS DESTROYED. ONE PERSON WAS INJURED WHEN THE HOUSE COLLAPSED.
AMATEUR RADIO RELAYED PUBLIC SAFETY RADIO REPORTS OF A COLLAPSED HOUSE NEAR BETHEL CHURCH ROAD AND US 27 IN HARALSON COUNTY.
HARALSON COUNTY 911 RELAYED REPORTS OF DOWNED TREES EAST OF FELTON AROUND 825 PM EST.
HARALSON COUNTY 911 RELAYED REPORTS OF DOWNED TREES EAST OF FELTON AROUND 825 PM EST. THIS IS DAMAGE ASSOCAITED WITH THE HARALSON/PAULDING COUNTY TORNADO. SEE THE 809 PM LSR FOR MORE DETAILS.

THE HARALSON TORNADO ENTERED NW PAULDING COUNTY NEAR YORKVILLE AROUND 828 PM. NUMEROUS ROADS WERE CLOSED TO DUE DEBRIS. THREE MILES SOUTH OF YORKVILLE THERE WAS AN OVERTURNED RV AND A BOX TRUCK WAS IMPALED BY A 2 BY 4. A HANGER WAS DESTROYED AT THE AIRPORT. FOUR PLANES WERE DAMAGED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED. IN ALL 27 HOMES WERE DAMAGED WITH SEVERAL HAVING MODERATE TO SEVERE DAMAGE.
AMATEUR RADIO RELAYED A SPOTTER REPORT OF A TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR YORKVILLE...OFF OLD YORKVILLE ROAD. DUAL POL RADAR DATA CONTINUES TO SHOW DEBRIS SIGNATURES WITH THIS STORM.
PAULDING COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES IN THE AREA 4 MILES WEST OF DALLAS AND TO THE AREA AROUND THE PAULDING COUNTY-NORTHWEST ATLANTA AIRPORT.

PAULDING COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES IN THE AREA 4 MILES WEST OF DALLAS AND TO THE AREA AROUND THE PAULDING COUNTY-NORTHWEST ATLANTA AIRPORT. NWS SURVEY CREWS DETERMINED THIS DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY A TORNADO THAT TRACKED ACROSS PAULDING COUNTY FROM JUST SW OF YORKVILLE TO THE NORTH DALLAS AREA.
GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED NEAR ACWORTH ON SUGAR MAPLE LANE.
CHEROKEE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RELAYED A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORT OF QUARTER-SIZED HAIL 2 MILES WEST OF WOODSTOCK AT 908 PM EST.
COBB COUNTY 911 REPORTED TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AT ROSWELL ROAD AND PROVIDENCE ROAD.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 100 MPH OCCURRED IN COBB COUNTY. THE PATH WAS 1.4 MILES LONG AND 150 YARDS WIDE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 913 PM AT THE INTERSECTION OF ROSWELL RD AND OLD CANTON RD...TRACKED EAST ALONG ROSWELL RD AND ENDED IN THE INDIAN RIDGE SUBDIVISION AT 915 PM. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND MINOR DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES OCCURRED ALONG THE PATH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT AN EF1 TORNADO WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 100 MPH OCCURRED IN COBB COUNTY. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ONE MILE WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 914 PM NEAR EAST COBB PARK ALONG ROSWELL RD. IT CONTINUED TO TRACK ALONG ROSWELL RD WHERE IT CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE TO A DAYCARE AND HOUSE NEAR PROVIDENCE RD AND ROSWELL RD. MINOR TREE AND ROOF DAMAGE CONTINUED JUST NORTH OF ROSWELL RD AND ENDED NEAR THE INDIAN RIDGE SUBDIVISION AT 915 PM.
DEKALB COUNTY 911 CENTER REPORTED POWER LINES DOWN ON DEKALB INDUSTRIAL WAY AND LAWRENCEVILLE HIGHWAY.
A BRANCH FLOWING INTO FAUSETT CREEK WASHED OUT A PORTION OF RAVEN CLIFF ROAD. DEBRIS BLOCKAGE AT THE CULVERT PIPE AND THE VOLUME OF WATER CAUSED THE BRANCH TO OVERFLOW THE ROCK BASED ROAD.

MINI COOPER DROVE INTO TWO FEET OF STANDING WATER ON HIGHWAY 17 JUST WEST OF HELEN. THE DRIVER HAD TO BE RESCUSED.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE FROM A EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS AROUND 100MPH. NUMEROUS TREES HAD THEIR TOPS SHEARED OFF ALONG WITH HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED AT THEIR TRUNKS. THE TORNADO LASTED 2 MINUTES AND TRAVELED 0.67 MILES AND HAD A WIDTH OF ABOUT 20 YARDS.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND DAMAGE FROM AN EF0 TORNADO IN TELFAIR COUNTY. THIS TORNADO SHEARED OFF THE TOPS OF SOFTWOOD TREES ALONG ITS 1.33 MILE PATH. THE WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 15 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST MOMENT.

NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND A SECOND SHORT LIVED TORNADO OCCURRED IN WHEELER COUNTY YESTERDAY. THIS TORNADO WAS ALSO CLASSIFIED AS AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS AROUND 100 MPH. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 0.24 MILES AND LASTED ONLY A MINUTE. THE TORNADO WAS 75 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST MOMENT. A HOUSE HAD SOME SHINGLE DAMAGE ALONG WITH ABOUT 50 TREES DOWNED.

WHEELER COUNTY EM REPORTED SOME DOWNED TREES ALONG WITH SOME TREES WITH SHEARED OFF TOPS. THIS CORRESPONDS WELL WITH THE RADAR ROTATION SIGNATURE. NWS SURVEY TEAM WILL INVESTIGATE THE DAMAGE TOMORROW.
